adrs.summary: Tigrbl needs a stable semantic center before it can support more transports, more runtimes, and more optimization work.
adrs.title: ADR-0001 - One Semantic Op Model

adrs.summary: The repo already has a strong macro-shape, but several architectural gaps come from boundary leakage: declarative layers owning runtime details, runtime owning concrete transport details, or concrete code bypassing compiled/runtime paths.
adrs.title: ADR-0002 - Package DAG and Ownership

adrs.summary: Tigrbl needs a single authoritative declarative layer so that execution, docs, hooks, and compilation are all driven from the same canonical objects.
adrs.title: ADR-0003 - Spec-First Authoritative Spec Layer

status_notes.note: Updated spec merge precedence to require generic monotone roll-up operators.
Updated spec merge precedence summary and monotone operator contract.

adrs.summary: a. framework hard defaults;
adrs.title: Specification merge precedence

status_notes.note: Updated to derive runtime semantics from scope channel and canonical event names.

adrs.summary: Tigrbl sits behind an ASGI-shaped server boundary. The architecture pack is explicit that richer semantics must be projected above `scope`, `receive`, and `send`, not by inventing a new server/app contract.
adrs.title: ADR-0005 - ASGI3 Boundary and Projection Rules

status_notes.note: Updated to keep bindings as selectors of contract classifications.
Updated binding model with per-binding app framing selection rules.
Updated protocol binding model with canonical BindingSpecs, profile/framing separation, and path-first ownership.

adrs.summary: Transport support in Tigrbl must scale horizontally. The architecture pack already normalizes REST, RPC, streaming, websocket, and WebTransport as bindings over the same semantic model.
adrs.title: ADR-0006 - Protocol Binding Model

status_notes.note: Updated support matrix to distinguish intended app framing from implemented support.

adrs.summary: Tigrbl needs an explicit statement of which protocols are first-class and what first-class means.
adrs.title: ADR-0007 - First-Class Protocol Support Matrix

adrs.summary: The kernel and runtime architecture already depend on a stable atom vocabulary. The registry pack and architecture notes enumerate structural atoms, handler atoms, and transport-related additions.
adrs.title: ADR-0008 - Atom Catalog and Atom Legality

adrs.summary: Tigrbl execution is phase-driven. The active line already has concrete phase names and a real runtime completion fence.
adrs.title: ADR-0009 - Phase Model and Phase Legality

status_notes.note: Updated from example, performance, and options test inventory to include hook lifecycle diagnostics and hookz traceability.

adrs.summary: Hooks are part of the framework contract, not a best-effort extension point. They need deterministic attachment, ordering, and scope rules.
adrs.title: ADR-0010 - Hook Model and Selector Semantics

adrs.summary: The architecture pack treats security dependencies as a distinct class that must execute before ordinary dependencies.
adrs.title: ADR-0011 - Security Deps and Deps Ordering

adrs.summary: Canonical operations depend on database and engine access, but unrestricted engine access inside arbitrary code paths leads to ownership leaks, hidden transaction semantics, and plan/runtime mismatch.
adrs.title: ADR-0012 - DB Guards and Transaction Guardrails

adrs.summary: Tigrbl needs a frozen baseline of canonical OLTP operations so that specs, bindings, handler atoms, engines, docs, and parity work all have the same semantic target.
adrs.title: ADR-0013 - Default Canonical OLTP Ops

status_notes.note: Accepted semantic parity update for JSON-RPC batch behavior and parity evidence.
Normalized ADR title while preserving accepted semantic parity body.

adrs.summary: REST and JSON-RPC are distinct transport/binding surfaces, but Tigrbl should not let them drift into different semantic frameworks. The architecture pack repeatedly treats them as different projections over the same semantic operations.
adrs.title: REST and JSON-RPC Semantic Parity

adrs.summary: Tigrbl needs a stable contract between canonical operations and engine implementations.
adrs.title: ADR-0015 - Engine Interoperability Contract

adrs.summary: The architecture pack already treats Tigrbl as a compiled-plan system rather than a purely interpreted request dispatcher.
adrs.title: ADR-0016 - Kernel Plan Compilation, Packing, Fusion, and Compaction

adrs.summary: The public authoring surface will continue to use decorators, but decorators are easy places to smuggle in hidden runtime behavior.
adrs.title: ADR-0017 - Decorators Are Declarative Sugar

status_notes.note: Updated from legacy package edge test inventory to govern module/package alias compatibility paths.

adrs.summary: Tigrbl needs shortcuts and aliases for usability, but shortcuts become dangerous when they express capabilities unavailable through the canonical surface or when they grow into independent feature trees. This ADR keeps convenience aligned with declarative canon.
adrs.title: ADR-0018 - Shortcuts and Aliases Expand to Canonical Forms

adrs.summary: The repo needs a reusable design pattern for moving from user-authored inputs to canonical framework objects and then to projections such as docs, bindings, plans, and runtime behavior.
adrs.title: ADR-0019 - Make / Define / Derive Pattern

adrs.summary: The architecture pack treats Rust as a real mirror target for specs, atoms, kernel, runtime, and ops, not as an ornamental acceleration layer.
adrs.title: ADR-0020 - Python / Rust Parity Contract

adrs.summary: The architecture and registry bundle already point toward a certification program rather than an informal feature sprint.
adrs.title: ADR-0021 - Certification Boundary and Promotion Gates

adrs.summary: Tigrbl is intended to remain ASGI-portable, but portability must be made concrete.
adrs.title: ADR-0022 - Tigrcorn / Uvicorn / Hypercorn Interop and Conformance

status_notes.note: Updated with contract-facing exchange aliasing.

adrs.summary: The repository exposes overlapping exchange tokens across Python bindings, runtime adapters, Rust specs, and architecture notes.
adrs.title: ADR-0023 - Exchange Model

status_notes.note: Updated to trace local families to contract families.

adrs.summary: After the semantic, binding, phase, and exchange layers are defined, Tigrbl still needs a stable runtime classification for units of work.
adrs.title: ADR-0024 - Runtime Family Model

adrs.summary: Once the runtime taxonomy is normalized, Tigrbl needs a runtime-owned transport abstraction so handlers and execution loops can remain transport-neutral where possible.
adrs.title: ADR-0025 - OpChannel and Runtime IO Abstraction

adrs.summary: The repository adopts `.ssot/registry.json` as the authoritative machine-readable truth model for planning, features, claims, tests, evidence, boundaries, releases, ADRs, and specs.
adrs.title: ADR-0001 Certification Truth Model

adrs.summary: Status: Proposed
adrs.title: Tigrbl ADR Set

adrs.summary: directly governed tests show that route registration, RPC exposure, and docs visibility are tightly coupled and must be governed by one binding-first source.
adrs.title: ADR-0027 - Binding-First REST Decorators and Route-Backed Spec Normalization

adrs.summary: The repository currently exposes convenience APIs through both `factories` and `shortcuts`. That boundary only stays coherent if one namespace owns construction helpers and the other acts purely as a convenience re-export layer.
adrs.title: ADR-0028 - Factories Are Canonical Construction Surfaces

adrs.summary: Documentation authority is partitioned by surface:
adrs.title: ADR-0029 - Documentation Support Uses Canonical Metadata Sources

adrs.summary: The current failing slice in `pkgs/core/tigrbl_tests/tests` shows that AppSpec-driven assembly, imperative harness assembly, kernel bootstrap, and Uvicorn-backed REST / JSON-RPC execution are drifting apart. The failures are not isolated smoke regressions; they indicate that multiple construction paths are no longer preserving the same compiled semantic outcome.
adrs.title: AppSpec, Harness, and Uvicorn Projections Preserve Canonical Outcomes

adrs.summary: Security parity tests cover both runtime and docs surfaces; parity requirements must be explicit at the decision level.
adrs.title: Status, Auth, and Error Projections Derive from Canonical Outcomes

adrs.body:
## Decision

Tigrbl canonical AppSpec authoring should be a nested, address-first spec tree for exposed surfaces, while concrete classes and decorators may remain ergonomic lowerers into that tree.

1. `AppSpec` is the root of the authored canonical spec tree.
2. `RouterSpec` groups address namespaces, middleware, nested apps, and path surfaces.
3. `PathSpec` owns concrete address paths, including resource paths, JSON-RPC dispatcher paths, WebSocket paths, SSE paths, WebTransport paths, docs UIX paths, docs payload paths, static mounts, and nested app mounts.
4. `TableSpec` may group table-scoped ops under a containing path without using concrete runtime model classes as canonical identity.
5. `OpSpec` owns semantic operation shape and inherits path/table context from the containing tree node instead of embedding concrete `model=...` or `table=...` runtime objects as canonical identity.
6. Protocol bindings select or invoke an op at the containing path; JSON-RPC bindings therefore receive their dispatcher path from the containing `PathSpec` and their method selector from the binding.
7. Documentation UIX and documentation payload paths are normal `PathSpec` entries; the inclusion policy for OpenAPI, OpenRPC, and JSON Schema artifacts is governed by a docs projection contract, not by the path alone. AsyncAPI generation, mounting, payload emission, and UIX are intentionally unsupported.
8. Engine binding is not path-owned. `AppSpec` owns the named engine inventory, while app, router, table, and op scopes attach to engines by name under the policy in `adr:1130`.
9. Transport scenario coverage is governed by the matrix contract in `spc:2152`; path ownership, protocol binding, framing, exchange, docs projection, static serving, and nested mounts remain separate layers.
10. Websocket binding semantics are governed by `adr:1131` and `spc:2153`: `subprotocols` describe WebSocket handshake negotiation, while `framing` describes post-handshake message interpretation.
11. The transport matrix is a governed SSOT coverage contract, not a new canonical spec graph entity or a required production module. Implementation belongs in the existing canonical spec layers.

## Context

Current discussion exposed two problems in the spec layer. First, concrete runtime classes such as ORM model classes leak into canonical examples through `model=Widget` and `table=Widget`. Second, docs UIX paths and docs payload paths need a way to decide which REST APIs, JSON-RPC methods, websocket surfaces, and other bindings are included in emitted docs artifacts.

The repository already has decisions for one semantic op model, protocol bindings, REST/JSON-RPC parity, endpoint-keyed transport routing, documentation support, and spec-layer authority. This ADR narrows the next planning slice: specify nested canonical AppSpec composition with path-scoped protocol bindings and docs projection selection, while keeping concrete classes as lowerers rather than canonical identity.

## Consequences

- `PathSpec` and docs projection contracts become the planning focus for the next implementation slice.
- Engine inventory and scope-level engine binding are governed separately from path and transport ownership.
- Transport support gaps such as stream path kinds, WS/WSS JSON-RPC symmetry, and WS/WSS NDJSON framing are tracked in an explicit matrix rather than inferred from existing binding classes.
- The matrix constrains implementation and testing, but `PathSpec`, binding specs, docs projection specs, `AppSpec`, `RouterSpec`, `TableSpec`, and `OpSpec` remain the implementation surfaces.
- Websocket support gaps are tracked through explicit binding fields for transport protocol, handshake subprotocols, and post-handshake framing.
- Docs UIX and docs payload surfaces do not need to become large independent authorities merely to expose `/docs`, `/openapi.json`, `/openrpc.json`, `/schemas.json`, or equivalent supported paths.
- AsyncAPI must remain absent from public docs helpers, canonical docs payload kinds, and system auto-mount behavior unless a future ADR explicitly reopens that surface.
- Documentation inclusion rules must be expressible in a projection spec that can include/exclude protocols, routers, paths, tables, op aliases, tags, and RPC methods.
- Existing concrete conveniences may continue to accept runtime classes, decorators, and shortcuts, but their canonical output must lower into a nested spec tree with stable spec-native table/resource identity.

## Rejected Alternatives

- Keep embedding REST path, JSON-RPC endpoint, and documentation projection policy directly on `OpSpec`.
- Treat docs UIX paths as the source of truth for OpenAPI/OpenRPC inclusion.
- Treat `PathSpec` as an engine binding scope.
- Conflate engine inventory with transport scenario coverage.
- Conflate websocket subprotocol negotiation with websocket message framing.
- Add a standalone production `transport_matrix` module as the authority for supported path kinds, protocol bindings, docs projection, static serving, nested mounts, or websocket framing/subprotocol semantics.
- Require graph refs for this slice when nested containment and inherited context are the desired authoring model.
- Treat concrete runtime classes as canonical table/op identity in the spec graph.

## Governed Feature Scope

- `feat:nested-appspec-pathspec-composition-001`
- `feat:path-scoped-protocol-binding-composition-001`
- `feat:docs-projectionspec-contract-001`
- `feat:docs-payload-projection-selection-001`
- `feat:docs-uix-projection-selection-001`
- `feat:shared-jsonrpc-dispatcher-table-crud-parity-001`
- `feat:nested-appspec-multisurface-corpus-fixtures-001`
- `feat:docs-projection-openapi-openrpc-selection-tests-001`
- `feat:canonical-spec-concrete-class-rejection-001`
- `feat:crud-rest-jsonrpc-shared-dispatcher-parity-corpus-001`
- `feat:appspec-engine-inventory-001`
- `feat:enginespec-name-identity-001`
- `feat:engine-scope-name-binding-001`
- `feat:engine-precedence-validation-001`
- `feat:concrete-engine-lowering-to-inventory-001`
- `feat:pathspec-engine-rejection-001`
- `feat:transport-scenario-matrix-001`
- `feat:transport-stream-pathkind-001`
- `feat:transport-ws-wss-jsonrpc-001`
- `feat:transport-ws-wss-ndjson-001`
- `feat:docs-transport-projection-selection-001`
- `feat:wsbindingspec-subprotocols-001`
- `feat:wsbindingspec-framing-001`
- `feat:wsbindingspec-framing-subprotocol-validation-001`
- `feat:ws-jsonrpc-subprotocol-parity-001`
- `feat:ws-ndjson-fail-closed-001`
- `feat:pathkind-websocket-binding-convergence-001`
- `feat:docs-websocket-transport-projection-001`

## Verification Plan

- Add AppSpec corpus fixtures that represent REST, JSON-RPC dispatcher, websocket, SSE, WebTransport, docs UIX, docs payload, static, nested app, middleware, and multi-table surfaces as a nested tree.
- Add engine inventory fixtures proving app/router/table/op engine bindings resolve by name with nearest-scope precedence and no path-owned engine field.
- Add transport matrix fixtures proving supported and planned/fail-closed transport scenarios are explicit.
- Ensure transport matrix fixtures exercise canonical spec entities directly rather than importing or requiring a standalone production matrix module.
- Add websocket binding fixtures proving `proto`, `subprotocols`, `framing`, and path kind convergence are explicit.
- Add fail-closed projection tests proving OpenAPI and OpenRPC include only the surfaces selected by the docs projection spec, and AsyncAPI remains unsupported.
- Add parity tests proving default CRUD table ops can lower into both REST paths and a shared JSON-RPC dispatcher path without embedding concrete model classes in canonical specs.

features.description: ACME worker task queues provide bounded capacity, timeout-aware enqueue/dequeue behavior, observable depth, and close semantics for operational backpressure.
features.title: ACME worker bounded queue

features.description: Tracks AliasBase as a first-class tigrbl_base contract surface.
features.title: AliasBase contract

features.description: Tracks AliasSpec as a first-class tigrbl_core._spec contract surface.
features.title: AliasSpec contract

features.description: Freeze the canonical analytical family to the declared aggregate and group_by verbs only. Python-local side of split Python/Rust feature ownership; Rust-side behavior is governed in tigrbl_rs.
features.title: Analytical family membership freeze

features.description: Keep runtime dispatch, docs builders, and default package exports aligned with the canonical analytical verb family. Python-local side of split Python/Rust feature ownership; Rust-side behavior is governed in tigrbl_rs.
features.title: Analytical runtime/docs/export parity

features.description: Project allow-anon behavior from canonical security metadata into runtime and generated docs consistently. Python-local side of split Python/Rust feature ownership; Rust-side behavior is governed in tigrbl_rs.
features.title: Anonymous access projection

features.description: Recover allow-anon behavior and REST create-status projection so runtime and docs derive from one canonical outcome, per adr/ADR-0031 and specs/status-auth-and-error-parity.md. Python-local side of split Python/Rust feature ownership; Rust-side behavior is governed in tigrbl_rs.
features.title: Anonymous access and REST create status parity

features.description: Tracks presence of the repository-root Apache 2.0 LICENSE file as a governance conformance feature.
features.title: Root Apache 2.0 license

features.description: Tracks router-level authentication dependency application through include_router compatibility.
features.title: API-level auth include-router compatibility

features.description: Tracks APIKey as a concrete first-class security dependency surface.
features.title: APIKey security dependency

features.description: Tracks apiKey security scheme documentation and runtime alignment through security scheme tests and OpenAPI security behavior coverage.
features.title: API key security docs/runtime alignment

features.description: App-framed message codec runtime target for protocol-runtime implementation.
features.title: App-framed message codec runtime

features.description: Require binding legality, profile legality, codec support, docs projection, and tests after alias normalization before Tigrbl claims an app-level framing as runtime-supported.
features.title: App-level framing runtime gating

features.description: Govern intended app-level framing support by canonical BindingSpec profile, family, exchange, and framing separately from current runtime support claims.
features.title: App-level framing support matrix

features.description: Tracks fail-closed validation for non-spec table and operation entries in AppSpec normalization.
features.title: AppSpec normalization input validation

features.description: Tracks AppBase as a first-class tigrbl_base contract surface.

features.description: Tracks the public operator projection of AppBase through the top-level tigrbl facade.

features.body:
Canonical documentation support includes both mounted documentation endpoints and framework-compatible in-memory publication surfaces that downstream tooling may inspect directly on the live app or router object.

For OpenAPI specifically, this feature covers publication of the current document through `openapi()` and compatibility exposure through `openapi_schema` when downstream consumers introspect the in-memory object instead of fetching `/openapi.json`.

The compatibility contract requires route mutations to invalidate stale cached OpenAPI state so the in-memory publication surface and the mounted JSON surface continue to represent the same document family for a given runtime-plan revision.

Evidence remains anchored in the mounted docs integration tests and the dedicated OpenAPI schema cache compatibility regression tests.
features.description: Tracks supported documentation outputs, metadata ownership, mounted docs surfaces, and framework-compatible in-memory publication for OpenAPI, OpenRPC, and JSON Schema. AsyncAPI is intentionally unsupported.
features.title: Canonical documentation support surfaces

features.title: Docs UIX projection selection

features.body:
This feature tracks symmetric WS and WSS JSON-RPC binding declarations, including explicit `framing=jsonrpc` semantics and JSON-RPC-compatible websocket subprotocol handling.

It is intentionally scoped to binding parity and validation on the active line. Frozen current-target certification remains a separate support-level question governed by the support matrix and release boundary documents.
features.description: WS and WSS JSON-RPC scenarios use symmetric binding declarations and active-line support; frozen current-target certification remains governed separately.
features.title: WS and WSS JSON-RPC subprotocol parity

features.description: WS and WSS NDJSON framing scenarios are explicit and fail closed until implementation support exists.
features.title: WS and WSS NDJSON fail-closed framing support

features.body:
This feature tracks the canonical declaration of websocket post-handshake message framing independently from handshake subprotocol negotiation.

The canonical framing vocabulary is broader than the current mature runtime framing-atom closure. This feature therefore covers declaration and normalization semantics rather than implying that every declared websocket framing token is already a first-class runtime surface.
features.description: Tracks WebSocket binding framing declaration through the WebSocketBindingSpec canonical model and Ws/Wss semantic aliases. Runtime support for each app-level framing token remains governed by target-set and runtime-gating rows.
features.title: WebSocket binding message framing declaration

features.title: WebSocket framing and subprotocol compatibility validation

features.description: WsBindingSpec declares handshake subprotocol tokens separately from message framing.
features.title: WebSocket binding subprotocol declaration

issues.description: Closed for the explicit 0.3.19 certification boundary: unfinished active-line rows remain tracked outside the frozen candidate boundary, while in-boundary rows are implemented and covered.
issues.title: Active line certification closure is blocked

issues.description: The concrete defineAppSpec and defineRouterSpec surfaces do not expose core canonical fields such as engines, engine_name, paths, docs payload/UIX nodes, response defaults, and related canonical graph structure. This forces authors back toward legacy flat composition and prevents the canonical graph from being the normal authoring path.
issues.title: Concrete AppSpec and RouterSpec factories cannot express the canonical graph

issues.description: DocsProjectionSpec, DocsPayloadSpec, and DocsUixSpec exist as canonical spec entities, but OpenAPI and OpenRPC builders still infer inclusion from runtime routes and models instead of applying projection policy from the canonical graph. Docs payload and docs UIX path nodes remain spec-only rather than authoritative.
issues.title: Docs projection specs are not wired to OpenAPI and OpenRPC emission

issues.description: AppSpec.engines, engine_name validation, and nearest-scope precedence exist in tigrbl_core, but concrete traversal and runtime resolution still read legacy raw engine attributes and ignore canonical engine inventory during materialization. Multi-engine AppSpec scenarios are therefore not runtime-true.
issues.title: AppSpec engine inventory and engine_name precedence are not runtime-authoritative

issues.description: OpSpec still carries legacy transport-shaping fields such as expose_routes, expose_rpc, http_methods, and path_suffix while the canonical design is path-owned and binding-owned. This overlap keeps lowering logic model-centric and weakens canonical graph clarity even when validation succeeds.
issues.title: Legacy op-owned transport fields overlap the path-owned canonical graph

issues.description: tigrbl_core defines RouterSpec.paths, PathSpec kinds, and docs/static/mount nodes, but the concrete app and runtime do not lower that path-owned canonical graph into executable REST, JSON-RPC, stream, SSE, websocket, webtransport, docs, static, or mount surfaces. The canonical graph currently validates without serving as the execution graph.
issues.title: Canonical PathSpec graph is not lowered into concrete runtime surfaces

issues.description: Planned transport and docs projection tests exist for REST, HTTP JSON-RPC, stream, SSE, websocket, WSS, WS JSON-RPC, WSS JSON-RPC, WS NDJSON fail-closed, WSS NDJSON fail-closed, and WebTransport, but the concrete lowering path is not closed enough for those scenarios to certify spec-to-runtime parity. The AppSpec transport matrix therefore remains incomplete as an implemented boundary.
issues.title: Canonical AppSpec transport matrix is not closed through runtime parity

issues.description: Package buildability/importability and package-coordinate traceability now have implemented features, published claims, passing tests, and passed evidence. The broader whole-repo certification gap remains tracked by the whole-repo gap report and by the xfail/runtime closure blocker.
issues.title: Certifiable package proof chain gap

issues.description: Against a service built on tigrbl==0.4.0 and observed tigrbl_runtime==0.1.12.dev1, POST /rpc calling Tenant.list without an Authorization header returns HTTP 200 with a JSON-RPC result payload. If generated JSON-RPC methods are protected by default, unauthenticated calls should fail with an authentication or authorization error. If generated JSON-RPC methods are public by default, that public policy must be explicit in OpenRPC.
issues.title: Generated JSON-RPC read allows unauthenticated access

issues.description: Against a service built on tigrbl==0.4.0 and observed tigrbl_runtime==0.1.12.dev1, GET /tenant without an Authorization header returns HTTP 200 OK with tenant records. If generated CRUD routes are protected by default, the request should fail with 401 or 403. If generated CRUD routes are public by default, that public policy must be explicit in the generated contract.
issues.title: Generated REST read allows unauthenticated access

issues.description: JSON-RPC table-backed CRUD dispatch allows Thing.create-style requests with missing required create params to reach persistence, causing database NOT NULL failures and leaking SQLAlchemy/DBAPI details, SQL text, table/column names, and bound parameters in the JSON-RPC -32602 response. Framework must validate generated operation input before persistence and sanitize persistence exceptions at the JSON-RPC boundary.
issues.title: JSON-RPC create with missing required fields leaks persistence internals

issues.description: Against a service built on tigrbl==0.4.0 and observed tigrbl_runtime==0.1.12.dev1, /openapi.json exposes generated CRUD operations with components.securitySchemes absent and zero operations carrying security requirements. Runtime and contract policy must agree: protected operations should emit security schemes and requirements; public operations should be explicitly limited and documented.
issues.title: OpenAPI generated operations do not declare authentication

issues.description: Against a service built on tigrbl==0.4.0 and observed tigrbl_runtime==0.1.12.dev1, /openrpc.json exposes generated JSON-RPC methods with an empty components.securitySchemes object and zero methods carrying security requirements. Runtime and OpenRPC contract policy must agree: protected methods should declare the security scheme and requirement; public methods should be explicitly identified as unauthenticated.
issues.title: OpenRPC generated methods do not declare authentication

issues.description: Direct package-coordinate feature rows now exist for every active package coordinate under pkgs; the remaining work is broader package-level claim/test/evidence depth, not missing coordinate traceability.
issues.title: Package coordinate feature traceability gap

issues.description: Downstream tests pass but emit repeated PydanticDeprecatedSince20 warnings because Tigrbl schema generation can pass legacy example metadata directly to pydantic.Field instead of json_schema_extra. This is a Pydantic v2 compatibility issue and a likely Pydantic v3 break if left unresolved.
issues.title: Pydantic v2 Field example deprecation warning

issues.description: Published tigrbl==0.4.1.dev3 declares unpinned split-package dependencies, so a normal pip install can resolve the facade package at 0.4.1.dev3 while split packages such as tigrbl-core, tigrbl-base, tigrbl-concrete, tigrbl-runtime, and related packages resolve to 0.4.0. In that environment import tigrbl fails with ImportError: cannot import name DocsPayloadKind from tigrbl_core._spec. The release lane must pin or otherwise coordinate split package versions so the public facade imports consistently from installed artifacts.
issues.title: tigrbl 0.4.1.dev3 resolves inconsistent split package imports

issues.description: The split xfail closure boundaries are frozen, but certification is blocked until selected xfail tests pass without xfail markers and linked features are implemented.
issues.title: Tigrbl xfail closure implementation blocked

specs.body:
## Intent

Define the next-slice contract for nested canonical AppSpec composition, path-scoped protocol bindings, and documentation projection selection.

This contract is not only a validation shape. The nested canonical tree is the
authority that concrete lowering, docs emission, runtime compilation, and
conformance checks must consume.

## Contract

1. `AppSpec` MUST be able to describe a nested canonical tree whose public address surfaces are under `RouterSpec.paths` or equivalent router-owned path composition.
2. `PathSpec` MUST represent concrete address paths, including REST resource paths, JSON-RPC dispatcher paths, WebSocket paths, WSS+JSON-RPC paths, SSE paths, WebTransport paths, docs UIX paths, docs payload paths, static file mounts, and nested app mounts.
3. `TableSpec` entries nested under a `PathSpec` MUST provide table/resource context for contained `OpSpec` entries without requiring `OpSpec.table` to hold a concrete runtime class.
4. Canonical `TableSpec` identity MUST be spec-native, using fields such as `name`, `resource`, and optional `model_ref`; concrete runtime model classes are lowerer inputs, not canonical identity.
5. `OpSpec` entries nested under a table or path inherit containing context and define semantic operation fields such as alias, target, arity, schemas, lifecycle defaults, and response policy.
6. `HttpRestBindingSpec` nested under an op selects the op by HTTP method at the containing `PathSpec` path.
7. `HttpJsonRpcBindingSpec` nested under an op selects the op by JSON-RPC method at the containing dispatcher `PathSpec` path; multiple table groups may share one dispatcher path.
8. WebSocket, WSS+JSON-RPC, SSE, and WebTransport bindings follow the same rule: the containing `PathSpec` owns the address path, and the binding owns the protocol-specific selector or stream semantics.
8a. `WsBindingSpec.subprotocols` owns WebSocket handshake negotiation, while `WsBindingSpec.framing` owns post-handshake message interpretation under `spc:2153`.
9. `DocsProjectionSpec` MUST define documentation inclusion policy separately from docs UIX and payload path exposure. It MUST be able to include and exclude by protocol family, router/path scope, table/resource scope, op alias/target, tags, visibility, and RPC method selector.
10. `DocsPayloadSpec` is a thin payload emission surface that names a supported documentation artifact kind such as OpenAPI, OpenRPC, or JSON Schema and points at a docs projection policy. AsyncAPI is intentionally unsupported and MUST NOT be accepted as a docs payload kind.
11. `DocsUixSpec` is a thin UIX surface that names a UI renderer such as Swagger, Lens, Redoc, Scalar, or equivalent and points at a docs payload path or docs projection policy.
12. Docs UIX paths and docs payload paths MUST be ordinary `PathSpec` entries. They are uniquely spec'd only when their renderer, payload kind, or projection policy differs.
13. OpenAPI emission MUST be selected from the canonical tree through a docs projection that includes appropriate HTTP/REST bindings and excludes non-selected protocol surfaces. Implementations MUST NOT treat incidental mounted runtime routes as the authority for OpenAPI inclusion.
14. OpenRPC emission MUST be selected from the canonical tree through a docs projection that includes appropriate JSON-RPC bindings and RPC method selectors, including shared dispatcher paths. Implementations MUST NOT infer OpenRPC inclusion only by walking runtime route attachment.
15. Concrete class conveniences, decorators, and imperative assembly helpers MAY remain non-flat and ergonomic, but their canonical output MUST normalize to this nested tree before docs generation, runtime compilation, or conformance checks consume it.
16. Concrete lowering MUST materialize runtime REST, JSON-RPC, stream, SSE, websocket, WebTransport, supported docs payload, docs UIX, static, and mount surfaces from this canonical tree. Reconstructing those surfaces from legacy model/router state after canonical lowering is invalid.
16a. Concrete lowering MUST fail closed for AsyncAPI docs payloads and MUST NOT mount `/asyncapi.json` or expose AsyncAPI UIX helpers.
17. Engine binding MUST follow `spc:2151`: `AppSpec` owns named engine inventory, app/router/table/op scopes attach by engine name, and `PathSpec` does not own engine binding.
18. Transport scenario coverage MUST follow `spc:2152`: path kind, protocol binding, exchange, framing, docs projection, static, and mount coverage are tracked as an explicit matrix.
19. Websocket path kinds MUST converge with websocket binding declarations: generic websocket path kinds allow baseline websocket framing, JSON-RPC path kinds require JSON-RPC framing, and NDJSON path kinds remain planned/fail-closed until supported.

## Examples

A table with default CRUD parity may lower into REST collection/member paths and a shared JSON-RPC dispatcher path:

```python
RouterSpec(
    paths=(
        PathSpec(
            path=/widgets,
            tables=(TableSpec(name=Widget, resource=widget, ops=(...REST collection ops...)),),
        ),
        PathSpec(
            path=/widgets/{item_id},
            tables=(TableSpec(name=Widget, resource=widget, ops=(...REST member ops...)),),
        ),
        PathSpec(
            path=/rpc,
            tables=(
                TableSpec(name=Widget, resource=widget, ops=(...JSON-RPC Widget ops...)),
                TableSpec(name=Account, resource=account, ops=(...JSON-RPC Account ops...)),
            ),
        ),
    )
)
```

Docs paths are ordinary path entries whose inclusion policy is delegated to docs projections:

```python
PathSpec(
    path=/openapi.json,
    docs_payload=DocsPayloadSpec(kind=openapi, projection=public-http),
)
PathSpec(
    path=/openrpc.json,
    docs_payload=DocsPayloadSpec(kind=openrpc, projection=public-rpc),
)
PathSpec(
    path=/docs,
    docs_uix=DocsUixSpec(kind=swagger, payload_path=/openapi.json),
)
```

## Traceability

- ADRs: `adr:1129`, `adr:1130`, `adr:1052`, `adr:1080`, `adr:1014`, `adr:1051`
- Companion specs: `spc:2151`, `spc:2152`, `spc:2153`
- Defines features: `feat:nested-appspec-pathspec-composition-001`, `feat:path-scoped-protocol-binding-composition-001`, `feat:docs-projectionspec-contract-001`, `feat:docs-payload-projection-selection-001`, `feat:docs-uix-projection-selection-001`, `feat:shared-jsonrpc-dispatcher-table-crud-parity-001`, `feat:nested-appspec-multisurface-corpus-fixtures-001`, `feat:docs-projection-openapi-openrpc-selection-tests-001`, `feat:canonical-spec-concrete-class-rejection-001`, `feat:crud-rest-jsonrpc-shared-dispatcher-parity-corpus-001`, `feat:pathspec-engine-rejection-001`, `feat:docs-transport-projection-selection-001`, `feat:pathkind-websocket-binding-convergence-001`, `feat:docs-websocket-transport-projection-001`

## Verification Plan

- Add positive corpus fixtures for multi-middleware, nested app, multi-table, REST, JSON-RPC, WSS+JSON-RPC, multiple SSE, WebTransport, docs UIX, docs payload, and static path surfaces.
- Add engine inventory fixtures through `spc:2151` to prove engine binding does not belong to `PathSpec`.
- Add transport matrix fixtures through `spc:2152` to prove every supported and planned/fail-closed transport scenario is explicit.
- Add websocket path kind convergence fixtures through `spc:2153` to prove websocket bindings and containing paths agree.
- Add negative corpus fixtures that reject concrete runtime model objects as canonical table/op identity in normalized specs.
- Add OpenAPI/OpenRPC projection tests proving docs inclusion is determined by `DocsProjectionSpec`, not by incidental mounted paths.
